title: Red Sox players marvel at ‘electric’ Fenway Park atmosphere during recent winning streak
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Morning Sports Update "The dugout's alive, and you still feel it in there.
" Caleb Durbin celebrates in the dugout after his clutch home run in the Red Sox win on July 20.
AP Photo/Charles Krupa By Hayden Bird July 21, 2026 | 10:47 AM 3 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Woman allegedly fires gun in the air after argument at Mass. beach
author: John Waller
bullets: Crime The 36-year-old woman fired the handgun out the driver's-side window of her Honda CRV, witnesses said.

By John Waller July 21, 2026 | 10:43 AM 1 minute to read A 36-year-old Dracut woman is facing charges after she allegedly got into an argument at Willowdale Beach in town on Sunday evening and then fired a handgun into the air out her driver’s-side window before driving away.

Jessica Morrow was arrested and charged with discharging a firearm within 500 feet of a building, disturbing the peace, carrying a firearm without a license, and have an unregistered motor vehicle, Dracut police said in a release.

title: Boston City Councilor Henry Santana hospitalized
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Santana is undergoing testing at Brigham and Women’s Hospital and will be away from City Hall for an extended period of time.
Boston City Councilor Henry Santana.

Lane Turner/Boston Globe Boston City Councilor Henry Santana was hospitalized for an extended period of time with an unspecified health condition, and remains under the care of doctors at Brigham and Women’s Hospital, he said in a statement issued Tuesday.

title: A 127-foot yacht runs aground off the Cape
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The luxury charter yacht had 13 people on board when it ran aground off the coast of Woods Hole in Falmouth.
A 127-foot luxury charter yacht ran aground on the rocks in the Woods Hole Pass, a narrow marine strait connecting Buzzards Bay and Vineyard Sound, on Friday, according to the U.
S.

title: The 10-day Red Line shutdown has begun. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Transportation The Red Line will be closed between Alewife and Park Street stations from July 21 through July 30.
Red Line service will be suspended between Alewife and Park Street for 10 straight days.
David Ryan/Boston Globe With the World Cup and Sail Boston now in the rear view mirror, the MBTA has launched its latest line closure to facilitate repairs and upgrades.

title: Boston police looking to identify man who allegedly assaulted Downtown Crossing worker
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The suspect allegedly punched and kicked the worker before placing him in a headlock, police say.
A photo of the suspect accused of physically and verbally assaulting an employee in the area of Downtown Crossing.
Boston Police Department Boston police are seeking the public’s help in identifying a man who is suspected of physically assaulting an employee in the Downtown Crossing area on Friday.

title: Red-hot Red Sox extend winning streak to 14, topping Orioles 6-5
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Boston Red Sox The Red Sox would match a club record with a win Tuesday night.
Boston won 15 straight in 1946 on a team led by future Hall of Famer Ted Williams.
Jarren Duran (left) greets Caleb Durbin at home plate after Durbin hit a solo home run to give the Red Sox the lead in the eighth inning.

title: Cyclist, 83, hit and killed by pickup truck on the Cape
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The victim, whose identity has not been released, died in the hospital.
